payload.title: Adenocarcinoma (Lung) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 65‑year‑old smoker with a 2 cm peripheral lung nodule.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this nodule malignant and, if so, what type?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows gland‑forming tumor with lepidic growth along alveolar septae. Tumor cells have enlarged nuclei, prominent nucleoli and produce intracellular mucin. Alveolar spaces may be filled with mucin pools.

sketch: Absence of keratin pearls or intercellular bridges
payload.pitfall: Collapse of alveolar spaces on frozen can simulate invasion; mucin lakes can be misinterpreted as necrosis.
payload.consequence: Overcalling invasion can prompt unnecessary lobectomy; undercalling carcinoma may leave residual disease requiring repeat surgery.
payload.action: Report as 'adenocarcinoma pattern suspected; recommend permanent sections and immunohistochemistry for confirmation.'

payload.title: Cholangiocarcinoma Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 65‑year‑old with jaundice and hilar bile duct stricture.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this bile duct lesion malignant or benign?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows gland‑forming tumor with desmoplastic stroma infiltrating the bile duct wall. The glands are irregular and lined by cuboidal to columnar cells with nuclear atypia and mitoses. Mucin production is common.

sketch: Irregular infiltrating glands in fibrous stroma
payload.pitfall: Benign peribiliary glands and reactive atypia can mimic carcinoma; sclerosing cholangitis may produce fibrosis without invasion.
payload.consequence: False positive diagnosis may result in unnecessary extensive resection; false negative may miss a curable cancer.
payload.action: Report presence or absence of infiltrating glands; when uncertain, describe atypia and defer to permanent sections and immunostains.

payload.title: Chromophobe Renal Cell Carcinoma Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 64‑year‑old with 5 cm renal mass suspicious for oncocytoma or chromophobe carcinoma.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this chromophobe carcinoma, oncocytoma, or other lesion?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows large polygonal cells with pale eosinophilic cytoplasm and prominent cell borders. Perinuclear clearing (halo) and wrinkled ('raisinoid') nuclei are characteristic.

payload.pitfall: Oncocytoma can show similar features but lacks perinuclear halos; frozen artifact may exaggerate cytoplasmic clearing.
payload.consequence: Misclassification can alter surveillance strategy as chromophobe RCC has different behavior than oncocytoma.
payload.action: Describe features and suggest chromophobe RCC when classic; recommend correlation with permanents and immunohistochemistry (CK7, CD117).

payload.title: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 55‑year‑old with 4 cm enhancing renal cortical mass.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is the margin free of clear cell carcinoma and is this lesion malignant?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows nests and sheets of cells with abundant clear cytoplasm and distinct cell borders separated by a delicate vascular network (chicken‑wire vasculature). Nuclei vary from small round (low grade) to pleomorphic with macronucleoli (high grade).

sketch: Sheets of clear cells with distinct borders
payload.pitfall: Oncocytoma and chromophobe RCC may show clear cells focally; frozen artifact can cause clearing of cytoplasm in other tumors.
payload.consequence: Misclassification affects extent of resection and need for further surgery.
payload.action: Report presence of clear cell carcinoma at margin if identified; when uncertain, describe features and defer to permanent sections and immunostains.

payload.title: Cribriform Carcinoma (Breast) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 60‑year‑old with palpable breast mass and microcalcifications.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lesion invasive cribriform carcinoma or in situ disease?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows nests of tumor cells with well‑defined borders forming sieve‑like (cribriform) spaces. The lumina are round and punched‑out, giving a 'Swiss cheese' appearance. Invasive carcinoma infiltrates stroma without a myoepithelial layer.

sketch: Absence of myoepithelial cells in invasive foci
payload.pitfall: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) with cribriform pattern retains a myoepithelial layer and may be confined within ducts; differentiating on frozen can be challenging.
payload.consequence: Underdiagnosis of invasion can lead to inadequate margins; overdiagnosis may lead to overtreatment.
payload.action: Comment on cribriform architecture and presence/absence of myoepithelial cells; recommend permanent sections and immunohistochemistry to confirm invasion.

payload.title: Desmoplastic Stroma Modal (Pancreas)

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 70‑year‑old with obstructive jaundice; intraoperative frozen of pancreatic margin shows dense fibrosis.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Does this desmoplastic stroma represent invasive carcinoma or chronic pancreatitis?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section reveals dense, hyalinized stroma with scattered spindled fibroblasts. No obvious glandular structures are seen. Chronic pancreatitis shows lobular atrophy and interlobular fibrosis with residual small ducts.

sketch: Residual acinar tissue in chronic pancreatitis
Compare with invasive glands in carcinoma
payload.pitfall: Frozen artifact may obscure small infiltrating glands; desmoplastic stroma alone is not diagnostic of carcinoma.
payload.consequence: Misinterpreting fibrosis as carcinoma leads to unnecessary extension of resection; missing small invasive glands leaves tumor behind.
payload.action: If no glands are identified, report as 'fibrotic/desmoplastic stroma, no definitive carcinoma identified'; recommend thorough sampling and permanent sections.

payload.title: Ductal Lesion (Pancreas) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 45‑year‑old with chronic pancreatitis and dilated pancreatic duct on imaging.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this duct lesion benign (PanIN) or carcinoma in situ?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows dilated ducts lined by epithelial cells with varying degrees of atypia. Low‑grade PanIN exhibits tall columnar mucinous epithelium with minimal atypia, whereas high‑grade PanIN demonstrates cribriform architecture and marked nuclear atypia without stromal invasion.

sketch: Dilated duct with tall columnar epithelium
Cribriform epithelial proliferation in high‑grade lesions
payload.pitfall: Frozen sections may exaggerate nuclear atypia; reactive duct changes in chronic pancreatitis can mimic PanIN.
payload.consequence: Overcalling high‑grade dysplasia as invasive carcinoma may prompt unnecessary resection; undercalling can miss an opportunity for curative surgery.
payload.action: Classify as low‑, intermediate‑ or high‑grade intraepithelial neoplasia when possible; avoid diagnosing invasion on frozen; recommend permanent evaluation.

payload.title: Endometrioid Carcinoma (Uterus/Ovary) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 60‑year‑old with abnormal uterine bleeding and endometrial mass.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lesion endometrioid carcinoma and what grade?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows back‑to‑back glands resembling endometrial glands with confluent architecture. Low‑grade tumors demonstrate well‑formed glands with minimal atypia; high‑grade tumors show solid growth and marked atypia.

sketch: Solid growth areas in high‑grade lesions
payload.pitfall: Hyperplastic endometrium with atypia may mimic low‑grade carcinoma; frozen sections may under‑grade tumors.
payload.consequence: Undergrading may result in inadequate staging; overgrading can lead to overtreatment.
payload.action: Assign grade based on architectural and nuclear features; recommend correlation with permanents for final grade and depth of myometrial invasion.

payload.title: Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma (Glandular) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 62‑year‑old with jaundice and pancreatic head mass undergoing Whipple procedure.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is the resection margin free of invasive carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section of the pancreatic neck margin shows infiltrative, angulated glands embedded in desmoplastic stroma. The glands are haphazardly arranged, irregular in shape and size, and lined by atypical epithelial cells with enlarged nuclei and mitotic figures.

sketch: Haphazard infiltrative glands at the margin
payload.pitfall: Benign pancreatic ducts entrapped by inflammation can mimic carcinoma; freezing artifact may exaggerate nuclear atypia.
payload.consequence: False positive margin involvement leads to unnecessary additional resection; false negative may leave residual tumor requiring adjuvant therapy.
payload.action: If infiltrative glands are present, report 'invasive adenocarcinoma at margin' versus 'no invasive carcinoma identified'; when uncertain, defer to permanent sections.

payload.title: Glioma Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 30‑year‑old with seizures and frontal lobe mass.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lesion a low‑grade glioma or high‑grade glioblastoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section and crush prep show glial fibrillary background with scattered atypical astrocytic cells. Low‑grade gliomas exhibit mild atypia and low cellularity, whereas high‑grade gliomas show marked pleomorphism, microvascular proliferation and necrosis.

payload.pitfall: Frozen section may underrepresent cellularity; demyelinating lesions can mimic low‑grade gliomas.
payload.consequence: Accurate grading guides extent of resection and adjuvant therapy.
payload.action: Comment on features suggestive of low‑ versus high‑grade glioma; recommend correlation with MRI and permanent sections.

payload.title: Hepatocellular Carcinoma Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 58‑year‑old with chronic hepatitis C and a 4 cm hepatic mass.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this mass hepatocellular carcinoma versus regenerative nodule?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows trabecular pattern of hepatocyte‑like cells with increased nuclear‑to‑cytoplasmic ratio and thickened cell plates (>3 cells thick). Endothelial wrapping and absence of portal tracts are noted.

sketch: Endothelial cell wrapping around tumor plates
payload.pitfall: Macroregenerative and dysplastic nodules may show mild trabecular thickening but retain portal tracts; freezing artifact can obscure sinusoidal pattern.
payload.consequence: Underdiagnosing carcinoma delays definitive resection; overdiagnosing cirrhotic nodules as carcinoma may lead to unnecessary surgery.
payload.action: Describe features suggestive of hepatocellular carcinoma; recommend correlation with AFP, imaging and permanent sections.

payload.title: Lymphoma Modal (Head & Neck)

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 60‑year‑old with rapidly enlarging cervical lymph node and systemic B‑symptoms.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lymph node involved by lymphoma or metastatic carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows diffuse effacement of nodal architecture by sheets of atypical lymphoid cells. Depending on subtype, cells may be small and monotonous or large with prominent nucleoli. Sinusoidal architecture is lost.

sketch: Diffuse sheets of atypical lymphoid cells
Variation in cell size depending on subtype
payload.pitfall: Reactive hyperplasia can produce effacement and polymorphous infiltrates; freezing artifact can distort cell morphology.
payload.consequence: Misclassifying lymphoma as carcinoma may lead to inappropriate surgery; misdiagnosing reactive changes as lymphoma results in overtreatment.
payload.action: If lymphoma is suspected, recommend flow cytometry and permanent sections; avoid definitive subtyping on frozen section alone.

payload.title: Meningioma Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 55‑year‑old with dural‑based mass and headaches.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lesion a meningioma and does it show atypical or malignant features?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows whorled bundles of spindle cells with psammoma bodies and a collagenous matrix. Cells have ovoid nuclei with fine chromatin. Atypical meningioma shows increased cellularity, mitoses and necrosis.

payload.pitfall: Hemangiopericytoma and metastatic carcinoma can mimic meningioma; frozen may not accurately reflect mitotic index.
payload.consequence: Undergrading atypical meningioma may lead to recurrence; overcalling benign meningioma as atypical results in unnecessary aggressive treatment.
payload.action: Describe classical meningioma features and comment on cellularity and mitoses; recommend permanents for definitive grading.

payload.title: Metastatic Tumor (CNS) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 65‑year‑old with known lung carcinoma and new brain lesion.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this brain lesion metastatic carcinoma or primary glial tumor?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows well‑circumscribed lesion composed of cohesive epithelial cells forming glands and sheets, sharply demarcated from adjacent brain parenchyma. Tumor lacks glial fibrillary background.

sketch: Sharp demarcation between tumor and brain
payload.pitfall: High‑grade gliomas can have sharp borders; meningiomas can mimic metastasis when invasive.
payload.consequence: Distinguishing metastasis from primary glioma determines need for systemic workup and guides surgical extent.
payload.action: Report features suggestive of metastasis; recommend correlation with clinical history and immunohistochemistry.

payload.title: Metastatic Tumor (Liver) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 50‑year‑old with known colon cancer and new hepatic lesion.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this hepatic lesion metastatic adenocarcinoma or primary hepatocellular carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows glands with dirty necrosis infiltrating hepatic parenchyma. Tumor cells form cribriform and tubular structures distinct from surrounding hepatocytes. Background liver may be relatively uninvolved.

sketch: Sharp demarcation between tumor and hepatocytes
payload.pitfall: Poorly differentiated metastatic tumors can mimic hepatocellular carcinoma; cholangiocarcinoma may also resemble metastasis.
payload.consequence: Accurate classification determines need for hepatic resection versus systemic therapy.
payload.action: Describe features of metastatic adenocarcinoma; recommend immunohistochemistry on permanents to determine primary site.

payload.title: Mucinous Tumor (Ovary) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 50‑year‑old with large unilateral ovarian cystic mass.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this mucinous neoplasm benign, borderline, or invasive carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows multiloculated cyst lined by mucin‑secreting columnar epithelium. Borderline mucinous tumors display epithelial stratification, tufting, and mild atypia without stromal invasion. Invasive carcinoma exhibits complex glands and destructive invasion into the stroma.

sketch: Epithelial stratification and tufting in borderline tumors
payload.pitfall: Sampling error on frozen may miss foci of invasion; pseudomyxoma peritonei may originate from appendiceal tumors.
payload.consequence: Incorrect classification affects staging and extent of surgery.
payload.action: Assess for invasion in multiple sections; when in doubt between borderline and invasive, defer to permanent sections and recommend complete staging.

payload.title: Papillary Thyroid Carcinoma Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 32‑year‑old woman with a 1.5 cm thyroid nodule and cervical lymphadenopathy.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this papillary thyroid carcinoma and are margins free of tumor?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows papillary fronds lined by cells with enlarged overlapping nuclei, nuclear grooves, and intranuclear pseudoinclusions. Psammoma bodies are present.

sketch: True papillary architecture with fibrovascular cores
payload.pitfall: Hashimoto thyroiditis and reactive changes can show nuclear grooves; freezing artifact can create pseudoinclusions.
payload.consequence: False positive diagnosis may lead to unnecessary total thyroidectomy; false negative may delay definitive treatment.
payload.action: If features are definitive, report 'papillary thyroid carcinoma'; otherwise describe nuclear atypia and defer to permanent sections.

payload.title: Papillary Renal Cell Carcinoma Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 70‑year‑old with incidental 3 cm renal mass on CT.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this papillary renal cell carcinoma and are the margins free of tumor?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows papillary and tubulopapillary structures lined by cells with eosinophilic to basophilic cytoplasm. Foamy macrophages may fill papillary cores. Psammoma bodies can be present.

payload.pitfall: Necrotic areas in clear cell RCC can mimic papillary structures; metanephric adenoma may show papillary patterns.
payload.consequence: Accurate subtyping influences prognosis and follow‑up.
payload.action: Report papillary carcinoma when typical features present; when uncertain, describe architecture and recommend permanent sections and immunohistochemistry.

payload.title: Papillary Thyroid Carcinoma Metastasis (Head & Neck) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 35‑year‑old with known papillary thyroid carcinoma and enlarged lateral neck lymph node.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lymph node involved by metastatic papillary thyroid carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section of lymph node shows papillary structures and clusters of cells with nuclear grooves and pseudoinclusions. Psammoma bodies may be present. The background lymphoid tissue is replaced by tumor.

payload.pitfall: Benign thyroid inclusions in lymph nodes can contain follicular cells but lack papillary nuclear features; metastatic mucoepidermoid carcinoma can mimic papillary carcinoma.
payload.consequence: Accurate detection of metastasis guides extent of neck dissection.
payload.action: If features are classic, report metastatic papillary thyroid carcinoma; when uncertain, describe findings and defer to permanent sections and thyroglobulin immunostain.

payload.title: Complex Sclerosing Lesion (Breast) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 55‑year‑old with architectural distortion on screening mammogram.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this radial scar/complex sclerosing lesion or invasive carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows a central fibroelastotic nidus with entrapped tubules radiating outward in a stellate pattern. Tubules are lined by bland epithelium and myoepithelial cells.

payload.pitfall: Frozen artifact may obscure myoepithelial cells; invasive tubular carcinoma can mimic a radial scar.
payload.consequence: Mistaking a radial scar for carcinoma can prompt unnecessary resection; missing invasive carcinoma delays treatment.
payload.action: Report as 'complex sclerosing/radial scar' if classic features are present; recommend permanent sections and immunostains to exclude tubular carcinoma.

payload.title: Serous Tumor (Ovary) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 45‑year‑old with pelvic mass; frozen section requested during staging laparotomy.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this serous tumor benign, borderline, or invasive carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows papillary structures with hierarchical branching. Borderline tumors exhibit stratified epithelium with mild nuclear atypia and no stromal invasion, whereas invasive serous carcinoma shows destructive stromal invasion by glands or solid sheets.

payload.pitfall: Papillary structures in cystadenomas may mimic borderline tumors; freezing artifact can obscure subtle stromal invasion.
payload.consequence: Underdiagnosing invasion may lead to inadequate staging; overdiagnosing borderline tumors as carcinoma may prompt unnecessary aggressive surgery.
payload.action: Classify as benign, borderline or invasive based on presence of stromal invasion; when uncertain, defer to permanents and ensure adequate sampling.

payload.title: Small Cell Carcinoma (Lung) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 70‑year‑old with rapidly growing central lung mass and paraneoplastic syndrome.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lesion small cell carcinoma versus non‑small cell carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows sheets of small round to fusiform cells with scant cytoplasm, finely granular ('salt‑and‑pepper') chromatin and inconspicuous nucleoli. Nuclear molding and high mitotic activity are present.

sketch: Sheets of small blue cells with nuclear molding
Crush artifact characteristic of small cell carcinoma
payload.pitfall: Crush artifacts on frozen can obscure morphology; basaloid squamous carcinoma can mimic small cell carcinoma.
payload.consequence: Misdiagnosis may alter adjuvant therapy (chemoradiation versus surgery).
payload.action: If features are classic, report 'small cell carcinoma'. If uncertain, describe as 'high‑grade neuroendocrine carcinoma vs. basaloid carcinoma; recommend permanents and immunohistochemistry.'

payload.title: Solid/Spindled Thyroid Lesion Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 55‑year‑old with rapidly enlarging thyroid mass and compressive symptoms.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lesion medullary carcinoma, anaplastic carcinoma, or a benign spindle cell lesion?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows sheets of polygonal or spindled cells with granular cytoplasm and amyloid‑like stroma. In anaplastic carcinoma the cells are markedly pleomorphic with areas of necrosis.

payload.pitfall: Spindle cell variant of papillary carcinoma and benign Hurthle cell tumors can mimic medullary carcinoma; Congo red stain on frozen sections may help identify amyloid.
payload.consequence: Misclassification may alter extent of surgery and lymph node dissection.
payload.action: Describe as 'spindle/solid thyroid lesion'; comment on presence or absence of amyloid and necrosis; recommend permanent sections and immunohistochemistry for definitive typing.

payload.title: Squamous Cell Carcinoma (Lung) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 58‑year‑old with cough and hilar mass on imaging.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this mass squamous cell carcinoma?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows nests of polygonal cells with abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. Intercellular bridges and focal keratin pearl formation are present. Nuclear atypia is moderate to marked.

payload.pitfall: Keratinization may be focal or absent in poorly differentiated tumors; reactive squamous metaplasia can mimic carcinoma in airway lesions.
payload.consequence: Overcalling metaplasia as carcinoma leads to overtreatment; undercalling poorly differentiated squamous carcinoma delays definitive surgery.
payload.action: If definitive features are present, report 'squamous cell carcinoma'. Otherwise describe as 'squamoid lesion; recommend permanent sections for classification.'

payload.title: Squamous Cell Carcinoma (Head & Neck) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 58‑year‑old with ulcerated tonsillar mass and enlarged cervical lymph node.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lesion squamous cell carcinoma and are margins clear?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows nests of atypical squamous cells infiltrating the submucosa. Keratin pearl formation and intercellular bridges support well‑differentiated carcinoma; poorly differentiated tumors show solid sheets with necrosis.

payload.pitfall: Reactive atypia in chronic tonsillitis can mimic carcinoma; frozen artifact may obscure keratinization.
payload.consequence: Accurate identification of carcinoma influences extent of surgery and adjuvant therapy.
payload.action: If definitive features present, report 'squamous cell carcinoma'; otherwise describe atypia and defer to permanent sections for confirmation.

payload.title: Tubular Carcinoma (Breast) Modal

case_points.label: Clinical Vignette
case_points.text: 48‑year‑old with screening‑detected 1 cm breast mass.

case_points.label: IOC Request
case_points.text: Is this lesion invasive tubular carcinoma or sclerosing adenosis?

case_points.label: Description
case_points.text: Frozen section shows well‑formed angulated tubules infiltrating fibrous stroma. Tubules are lined by a single layer of bland epithelial cells with open lumina. Myoepithelial cells are absent.

sketch: Single layer of epithelial cells without myoepithelium
payload.pitfall: Sclerosing adenosis may mimic tubular carcinoma but retains myoepithelial cells; these may be hard to appreciate on frozen.
payload.consequence: Misdiagnosis may lead to overtreatment or undertreatment and affect margin assessment.
payload.action: Describe features and comment on presence or absence of myoepithelial cells; recommend permanents and immunostains when uncertain.
